sections.paragraph:
ὁμοφωνία, ας, ἡ (ὁμός ‘common’, φωνή; Aristot., Pol. 2, 5; Philo, Conf. Lingu. 6). From the basic mng. ‘state of harmony in expression’ (s. L-S-J-M s.v.) derives the fig. ext.: state of being in accord w. others, harmony (Ecphantus in Stob. 4, 7, 64) 1 Cl 51:2.—DELG s.v. φωνή.

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

L-S-J-M L-S-J-M = (L-S-J) HLiddell/RScott, A Greek-English Lexicon; New Edition by HSJones/RMcKenzie—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

ext. ext. = extension

Stob Stob , V a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 710
